description_long: This object consists of a group of four bladed weapons: two large sabers with curved, single-edged blades and ornate crossguards, and two straight, double-edged swords with simple crossguards and pommels. The sabers feature metallic blades, one with a clean finish and the other with a weathered, rusted appearance, and their guards are colored gold and bronze. The straight swords have dark, metallic blades and brown, wrapped handles with geometric pommels. The handles of the sabers are slightly curved and appear to be made of wood or leather, while the straight swords have cylindrical, ribbed grips. The overall composition suggests a display or crossed arrangement, with the sabers forming an 'X' and the straight swords flanking them. The materials and design evoke a historical or fantasy aesthetic, suitable for decorative or functional use.
description: A set of four bladed weapons: two ornate, curved sabers with gold and bronze guards, and two straight swords with simple, wrapped handles and dark blades.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four weapons: two crossed sabers with gold and bronze guards in the center, flanked by two straight swords with dark blades and simple handles.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profiles of the blades and the layered arrangement of the sabers and swords, with the guards and handles aligned.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the backs of the sabers and swords, with the same crossed and flanking arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blades and the alignment of the guards and handles from the other side.
attribute_blade_type: two curved, single-edged sabers; two straight, double-edged swords
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, cylindrical handles with geometric pommels; sabers have slightly curved grips, swords have straight grips
attribute_ornamentation: colored metallic guards and pommels; no engravings or inlays
